materialitySignals: new customer promises and visible workflows
new untrusted local archive and PNG format
new runtime pack schema and project archive compatibility version
new IndexedDB repositories, concurrency, retention, and capacity behavior
expanded Electron preload/main filesystem trust boundary

dimensions.definitionOf10: The update proves the Phase 4 outcome that compatible legally traceable humanoid content can grow independently of application releases, with named users, no-change consequence, evidence, and owner approval boundary.

Selected scope is the smallest coherent install+author+manage ecosystem promise; terrain, scenes, non-humanoids, remote distribution, executable content, editor, marketplace, and engine adapter are explicitly evaluated and excluded.

Shared pack/runtime/draft/archive/license contracts, repositories, renderer, project embedding, bridge v2, ownership, dependency direction, and rollout fit current UPD-001 architecture without hidden design decisions.

Every pack/draft/project state transition defines strict data, identity, transactions, concurrency, interruption, quota, removal, missing content, and rollback with stable errors and no partial mutation.

Correctness, durability, recoverability, security, legal traceability, compatibility, accessibility, performance, capacity, offline, maintainability, and distribution have measurable scenarios and reference boundaries.

Every promise/flow/behavior maps to actual-host, adversarial, performance, accessibility, compatibility, release, and rollback evidence with exact completion arithmetic and a truthful baseline entry condition.
A fresh implementer can execute nine dependency-gated packages without choosing product behavior, format semantics, storage topology, errors, security authority, compatibility, or UX recovery states.

Every view, command, dialog, status, failure, recovery, responsive layout, keyboard path, focus transition, naming rule, 200% reflow, target size, and reduced-motion behavior is explicit and testable.
Technical artists can create valid packages without JSON and implementers have deterministic formats, validation reports, fixtures, source ownership, and extension governance.
All local package, PNG, URL, project embedding, browser quota, Electron grant, reparse, executable-content, diagnostics, and provenance threats have bounded mitigations and pre-mutation fixtures.

Interrupted install/remove/draft/project operations retain last-known-good state, expose stable diagnostics and recovery, and support independently withdrawable local releases without invented server operations.
Pack, image, draft, storage, worker/concurrency, status cadence, main-thread, validation, preview, autosave, and quota boundaries are numeric and map to benchmark fixtures.

Existing locks/projects/archives/outputs remain unchanged; new pack/draft/project archive versions, embedded precedence, old-reader failure, registry upgrades, and rollback readers are exact and testable.
Web and portable Electron release together with reproducible artifacts, actual Pages/clean-machine gates, feature withdrawal rules, backward readers, and no local content deletion during rollback.

findings.title: Project archive v2 and old-reader contract was incomplete

evidence.claim: Round one lacked an exact manifest, version detection, embedded paths, limits, and validation order.

findings.problem: A non-bundled project could not be serialized or rejected by an old reader without implementation invention.
findings.impact: Cross-host project portability and no-data-loss compatibility were unimplementable.
findings.remediation: Defined strict manifest v2, literal detection, embedded records/paths, limits, validation order, bundled exclusion, and old-reader unsupported-version behavior.
findings.verification: Project archive v1/v2, old-reader, malformed embedded, limit, and bidirectional actual-host fixtures.

findings.title: Pack lock and package checksum semantics were ambiguous

evidence.claim: Round two identified potential conflict between existing canonical pack-document locks and new complete package identity.

findings.problem: Using one SHA field for two meanings could force silent project migration or mismatched embedded resolution.
findings.impact: Existing locks and cross-host hashes could drift.
findings.remediation: Kept PackLockRef.sha256 as canonical pack.json hash for all origins and separated complete package SHA-256 into installed/archive identity.
findings.verification: Existing lock hash fixtures, package/pack hash mismatch fixtures, and unedited project regressions.

findings.title: Draft repository and concurrency topology was underspecified

evidence.claim: Round one/two required physical stores, blob ownership, transaction sets, revision behavior, recovery, and deletion.

findings.problem: Draft persistence, quota recovery, and concurrent blob references required architectural guesses.
findings.impact: Draft work or source art could be lost or orphaned.
findings.remediation: Specified shared IndexedDB stores for both hosts, limits, immutable blob references, one owning transaction, first-commit-wins, post-commit deletion recheck, and deterministic recovery ZIP.
findings.verification: Web/Electron draft repository contract, stale-tab, quota, interruption, refcount, recovery export, and reopen tests.

findings.title: Embedded pack lifecycle and local enabled precedence was unclear

evidence.claim: Review required project-scoped validation/install semantics and destination-local enablement behavior.

findings.problem: Opening a transferred project could silently install or disable content differently by host.
findings.impact: Offline and cross-host parity could diverge.
findings.remediation: Embedded packages remain project-scoped, authoritative for that project, never globally install without action, preserve bytes, and ignore global disabled state for locked resolution.

findings.title: Maximum package resource strategy was missing

evidence.claim: A 64 MiB package and 10-second target require bounded scheduling.

findings.problem: Concurrency, memory, worker, progress, cancellation, and main-thread ownership were unspecified.
findings.impact: A compliant implementation could freeze or exhaust memory.
findings.remediation: Specified sequential streamed entries, max two PNG workers, per-asset decoded cap, cache ownership, progress cadence, cancellation, and 100 ms main-thread ceiling.
findings.verification: Maximum/one-over benchmark with memory, duration, progress, long-task, and cancellation capture.

findings.title: License profile extension and registry ownership was undefined

evidence.claim: A finite license vocabulary needs schema, source path, approval, exported wording, and unknown-profile behavior.

findings.problem: Hosts or future contributors could diverge or imply unreviewed legal support.
findings.impact: Pack validation and credits could become inconsistent or legally misleading.
findings.remediation: Defined LicenseProfile schema, one host-neutral module, unknown-profile failure, immutable package provenance, product-owner update governance, and no-legal-advice boundary.
findings.verification: Registry completeness, unknown/chosen-not-offered, attribution wording, host parity, and review-approval tests.

findings.title: Alpha-preserving token rendering was ambiguous

evidence.claim: Deterministic cross-host pixels require an exact rule for partially transparent mapped colors.

findings.problem: Hosts could replace or preserve semi-transparent RGB differently.
findings.impact: Preview/export hashes and author expectations would diverge.
findings.remediation: Defined RGB lookup for alpha 1..255, RGB replacement/fixed semantics, alpha preservation, alpha-zero output, no heuristic branch, and reference alpha fixtures.
findings.verification: Pure compositor and web/Electron pixel fixtures at alpha 1,64,128,254,255.

findings.title: Quota and partial draft commit recovery was incomplete

evidence.claim: Draft promises require retained in-memory edits, last commit, atomic rollback, and recovery export.

findings.problem: Storage exhaustion could expose partial drafts or lose unsaved source bytes.
findings.remediation: Defined atomic transaction visibility, dirty in-memory retention, last committed guarantees, safe cleanup/limits, and deterministic recovery artifact.
findings.verification: Quota at each transaction stage, process kill, cleanup, download recovery, and read-back fixtures.

findings.title: Meaningful draft edits were subjective

evidence.claim: Autosave needed exact triggers and non-triggers.

findings.problem: Teams could save transient preview state or miss durable edits.
findings.impact: Performance and reopen behavior would be inconsistent.
findings.remediation: Enumerated all identity/asset/mapping/provenance triggers and focus/selection/preview/filter/validation non-triggers.
findings.verification: Mutation classification table and autosave timing tests.

findings.title: Pack error topology and exhaustive stable mapping were absent

evidence.claim: Threats and recovery flows required exact shared HostError/ProductError integration.

findings.problem: Implementers could create nested or divergent error layers and unstable host behavior.
findings.impact: Adversarial parity and actionable recovery could not be tested.
findings.remediation: Added complete code registry, fixture mapping, direct HostErrorCode/ProductError union integration, warning behavior, and existing-host-code boundary.
findings.verification: Every adversarial fixture asserts the same top-level code and pre-mutation capture on both hosts.

findings.title: Electron pack grants and atomic reparse-safe I/O were incomplete

evidence.claim: New privileged methods needed exact grant ownership, scope, lifetime, path resolution, replacement, and rollback.

findings.problem: Renderer forgery or path replacement could expand filesystem authority.
findings.impact: Arbitrary read/write or TOCTOU failures were possible.
findings.remediation: Defined main-memory webContents-bound grants, scopes/expiry, reparse validation, temp sibling write/flush/read-back/hash, re-resolution, atomic replacement, rollback, and unsupported-filesystem failure.
findings.verification: Forged/wrong-owner/wrong-scope/stale grant and symlink/junction/TOCTOU/failure fixtures in packaged Electron.

findings.title: Bundled runtime adapter ownership and reversibility was unclear

evidence.claim: Unifying bundled primitives and installed sheets could accidentally branch by host/UI or change baseline pixels.

findings.problem: Adapter placement and old implementation oracle were unspecified.
findings.impact: Renderer duplication or baseline regression risk remained.
findings.remediation: Placed adapter in host-neutral pack-runtime, retained primitive oracle until exact hashes pass, and required rollback-compatible reader.
findings.verification: Dependency scan and all bundled frame/sheet/export/credit hash regressions.

findings.title: Shared license registry path remained implicit

evidence.claim: Round three found the registry schema but no single module path.

findings.remediation: Bound LICENSE_PROFILES to product/src/domain/license-profiles.ts and prohibited package/settings/network overrides.
findings.verification: Duplicate-literal scan and shared import tests.

findings.title: Draft recovery ZIP layout remained implicit

evidence.claim: Round three found a named recovery artifact without exact paths or import semantics.

findings.problem: Hosts could produce incompatible recovery files or imply unsupported restore.
findings.impact: Manual recovery and tests would be ambiguous.
findings.remediation: Defined manifest/draft/assets/report/README paths, canonical rules, checksum inventory, and explicit non-importable manual-recovery purpose.
findings.verification: Web/Electron deterministic recovery entry/hash/report fixture and Add-pack rejection test.

editsMade: Selected Phase 4 local humanoid pack ecosystem as the smallest coherent next promise and separated terrain and engine adapter updates.
Created validated 163-record traceability model with 4 promises, 12 scenarios, 24 capabilities, 11 flows, 40 behaviors, 12 exclusions, and 9 work packages.
Defined complete pack, runtime, PNG, provenance, license, draft, project archive v2, bridge v2, repository, error, security, performance, rollout, and rollback contracts.
Defined full product UX, interaction states, responsive behavior, content language, focus/keyboard, and accessibility requirements.
Updated top-level roadmap, MVP, North Star, landing, execution, manifest, and generated update documentation.
Repaired all round-one and round-two critical/high findings and both round-three clarifications directly in canonical specs.

implementationSequence: WP-UPD002-SPEC: owner review and approval of promises, decisions, exclusions, entry condition, and risks
WP-UPD002-CONTRACTS: schemas, canonical writers/readers, PNG/license validation, errors, adversarial fixtures
WP-UPD002-RUNTIME: bundled adapter, sheet runtime, pure compositor, unchanged baseline hashes
WP-UPD002-PACK-REPOSITORY: web/Electron install, versions, dependencies, remove, missing-lock recovery
WP-UPD002-AUTHORING: drafts, import, mappings, provenance, preview, recovery, validation/export/self-install
WP-UPD002-WEB-UX and WP-UPD002-DESKTOP-ADAPTER: actual host workflows and security boundaries
WP-UPD002-CROSS-HOST: pack and dependent project archive v2 parity
WP-UPD002-REGRESSION: offline, performance, accessibility, security, release, rollback, and UPD-001 regression
verificationGates: Traceability and generated update validators pass with no orphan/stale records
All named package, PNG, schema, license, bridge, filesystem, capacity, and concurrency fixtures return exact codes before mutation
All 11 new flows and 40 behaviors pass production web and packaged Electron actual-UI evidence
Bidirectional .spritepack and dependent project archive v2 round trips preserve pack/project/runtime/output/credit hashes
Actual Pages offline and packaged clean-machine/rollback gates pass with zero severity 0-2 issues

rounds.summary: Found missing or insufficiently explicit archive v2, draft storage, alpha rendering, credit merge, resource, license, embedded, quota, enablement, error, provenance, adapter, and autosave contracts.

Verified round-one repairs and exposed integration ambiguity in error topology, lock/package checksums, bundled embedding, final writer, transaction isolation, embedded precedence, grant/I/O safety, registry, and recovery details.
Confirmed all material contracts executable; requested only shared license registry path and deterministic draft recovery layout, both repaired before final record.

stabilization.productVisionAlignment: A local-first data-only content ecosystem lets compatible humanoid art grow independently of application releases while preserving exact locks, provenance, offline custody, and one shared web/Electron product core.
